headline: Snapple Launches iPhoan 17, Which Is Identical To iPhoan 16 But Costs Rs 40,000 More And Has A New Font
summary: Pre-orders sell out in 4 minutes. Analysts call it 'the best new font Snapple has ever kerned.'
fullBody: Snapple Inc. unveiled the iPhoan 17 at its annual September event in Cupertino, introducing what CEO Jim Rook called 'the biggest leap forward in iPhoan history' — a device that, according to a side-by-side comparison by The Splurge, is indistinguishable from the iPhoan 16 except for a slightly warmer notification tone, a new system font with marginally tighter kerning, and a starting price Rs 40,000 higher than last year's model. Pre-orders sold out globally in four minutes. Financial analysts issued a combined statement calling it 'the best kerning in consumer electronics history' and raising Snapple's price target by $40. The old font has been deprecated and will no longer be supported after iOS 20. Three people are already camping outside the Mumbai Snapple Store.
imagePrompt: Two identical phones side by side with a tiny font change highlighted, massive price difference labels, comic editorial illustration, Snapple-style presentation backdrop
